    A

    addRock(int, int) - Method in class Grid
    Marks a cell as blocked by a rock.

    C

    Command - Enum Class in Unnamed Package
    The three actions the player can ask the robot to perform.
    CommandResult - Class in Unnamed Package
    Holds the outcome of executing one game command (move or turn).
    CommandResult(boolean, boolean, String) - Constructor for class CommandResult
    Creates a result for one command execution.
    createSetup() - Method in class RandomLevelFactory
    Creates a new game setup without using terminal size (e.g.
    createSetup(TerminalSize) - Method in class RandomLevelFactory
    Builds the complete starting state for one game.

    D

    Direction - Enum Class in Unnamed Package
    The four compass directions the robot can face.

    E

    EAST - Enum constant in enum class Direction
     
    execute(Command) - Method in class RobotController
    Executes one command (move forward, turn left, or turn right) and returns the result.

    G

    GameSetup - Class in Unnamed Package
    Holds everything needed to start one new game: the grid, the robot, and the briefing message.
    GameSetup(Grid, Robot, String) - Constructor for class GameSetup
    Creates a game setup with the given grid, robot, and briefing text.
    getBriefing() - Method in class GameSetup
    Returns the briefing message to show the player at the start of the level.
    getColumn() - Method in class Position
    Returns the zero-based column index of this position.
    getColumns() - Method in class Grid
    Returns the number of columns in the grid.
    getDirection() - Method in class Robot
    Returns the direction the robot is currently facing.
    getGoal() - Method in class Grid
    Returns the goal position (charging station).
    getGrid() - Method in class GameSetup
    Returns the grid for this game.
    getGrid() - Method in class RobotController
    Returns the grid managed by this controller.
    getLabel() - Method in enum class Command
    Returns a short, user-friendly description of this command (e.g.
    getLabel() - Method in enum class Direction
    Human-readable label for this direction, used in the UI (e.g.
    getMessage() - Method in class CommandResult
    Returns the message to display to the player describing what happened.
    getMovesTaken() - Method in class Robot
    Returns how many successful forward moves the robot has made (turns are not counted).
    getName() - Method in class Robot
    Returns the robot's display name.
    getPosition() - Method in class Robot
    Returns the robot's current cell on the grid.
    getRobot() - Method in class GameSetup
    Returns the robot for this game.
    getRobot() - Method in class RobotController
    Returns the robot managed by this controller.
    getRow() - Method in class Position
    Returns the zero-based row index of this position.
    getRows() - Method in class Grid
    Returns the number of rows in the grid.
    getStatusLine() - Method in class Robot
    Builds a one-line status string for debugging or plain-console output (e.g.
    Grid - Class in Unnamed Package
    Represents the game board: size, obstacle (rock) positions, and goal cell.
    Grid(int, int, Position) - Constructor for class Grid
    Creates a grid with the given dimensions and goal position.

    H

    hasReachedGoal() - Method in class CommandResult
    Returns whether the robot has reached the charging station (goal) after this command.
    hasReachedGoal() - Method in class RobotController
    Returns whether the robot is currently on the goal (charging station) cell.
    hasRock(Position) - Method in class Grid
    Returns whether the given cell contains a rock (blocked).

    I

    isGoal(Position) - Method in class Grid
    Returns whether the given position is the goal (charging station) cell.
    isInside(Position) - Method in class Grid
    Checks whether the given position is within the grid bounds (0 to rows-1, 0 to columns-1).
    isSuccessful() - Method in class CommandResult
    Returns whether the command was applied successfully (move or turn was performed).

    M

    main(String[]) - Static method in class RobotGame
    Entry point: creates the terminal, starts the screen, and runs the game loop until the user quits.
    move(Position) - Method in enum class Direction
    Computes the position one step ahead in this direction from the given position.
    MOVE_FORWARD - Enum constant in enum class Command
     
    moveForward() - Method in class Robot
    Moves the robot one step forward in its current direction and increments the move counter.

    N

    NORTH - Enum constant in enum class Direction
     

    P

    peekForwardPosition() - Method in class Robot
    Computes the position one step ahead in the current facing direction, without moving.
    Position - Class in Unnamed Package
    Represents a single cell on the game grid by its row and column indices.
    Position(int, int) - Constructor for class Position
    Creates a position for the given row and column.

    R

    RandomLevelFactory - Class in Unnamed Package
    Creates a new random puzzle (grid, robot, briefing) each time the game starts or the player presses R.
    RandomLevelFactory() - Constructor for class RandomLevelFactory
     
    render(Robot) - Method in class Grid
    Builds a text representation of the grid with row/column headers and one character per cell (robot, goal, rock, or empty).
    Robot - Class in Unnamed Package
    Represents the robot's state: where it is, which way it faces, and how many moves it has made.
    Robot(String, Position, Direction) - Constructor for class Robot
    Creates a robot with the given name, starting position, and initial facing direction.
    RobotController - Class in Unnamed Package
    The game rule engine: decides whether moves are legal and executes commands.
    RobotController(Grid, Robot) - Constructor for class RobotController
    Creates a controller for the given grid and robot.
    RobotGame - Class in Unnamed Package
    The user-interface layer for the Robot Rescue Mission game.
    RobotGame() - Constructor for class RobotGame
     
    runScript(List) - Method in class RobotController
    Runs a list of commands one after another and prints each step to the console.

    S

    sameCell(Position) - Method in class Position
    Checks whether this position and the other refer to the same cell (same row and column).
    SOUTH - Enum constant in enum class Direction
     

    T

    toString() - Method in class Position
     
    translate(int, int) - Method in class Position
    Creates a new position by adding the given row and column deltas to this position.
    TURN_LEFT - Enum constant in enum class Command
     
    TURN_RIGHT - Enum constant in enum class Command
     
    turnLeft() - Method in enum class Direction
    Returns the direction obtained by turning left (counter-clockwise) from this direction.
    turnLeft() - Method in class Robot
    Turns the robot left (counter-clockwise) without changing position.
    turnRight() - Method in enum class Direction
    Returns the direction obtained by turning right (clockwise) from this direction.
    turnRight() - Method in class Robot
    Turns the robot right (clockwise) without changing position.

    V

    valueOf(String) - Static method in enum class Command
    Returns the enum constant of this class with the specified name.
    valueOf(String) - Static method in enum class Direction
    Returns the enum constant of this class with the specified name.
    values() - Static method in enum class Command
    Returns an array containing the constants of this enum class, in the order they are declared.
    values() - Static method in enum class Direction
    Returns an array containing the constants of this enum class, in the order they are declared.

    W

    WEST - Enum constant in enum class Direction
